slides.headline: Your track has no ISRC code?

slides.body: That's the first thing we fix at SyncMaster. Here's exactly why it matters more than almost anything else in your catalogue. ↓

A unique identifier assigned to each individual recording.
Think of it as a barcode for your music.
Why it matters for sync specifically:

→ It identifies your recording in global licensing databases
→ It links your track to royalty collection societies worldwide (SAMRO, COSON, SOCAN, PRS, ASCAP, and more)
→ Without it, performance royalties from any placement can't reach you
→ Without it, your track can't be properly catalogued in sync libraries A placement without an ISRC = money you'll never collect. HOW TO GET ONE: Through your national music body:
→ RISA (South Africa)
→ COSON (Nigeria)
→ MCSK (Kenya)
→ CAPASSO (South Africa)
→ GHAMRO (Ghana) Through your distributor:
→ DistroKid, TuneCore, CD Baby all assign ISRCs automatically Direct registration: IFPI.org Cost: Free or minimal.
Time: 24–72 hours in most cases. Every track in the SyncMaster catalogue has an ISRC.
Every composer we onboard gets this sorted in the first call. Questions? DM us. Or apply: link in bio. #ISRC #MusicRights #SyncReady #AfricanComposers #SyncMaster
